description: GAAP-aligned journal-entry controls: double-entry balance enforcement, closed-period protection, materiality review of manual adjustments, and a compliance score trail. Includes a schema template so accounting systems (or extracted documents) can submit entries through a connector.
name: GAAP Journal-Entry Controls

entities.description: A general-ledger journal entry submitted for posting.

events.description: The journal entry violates a posting control (unbalanced, or targets a closed period) and is held for correction.

rules.name: Hold unbalanced journal entries
rules.description: Double-entry accounting: total debits must equal total credits. An unbalanced entry is held, never silently corrected.
rules.reasonTemplate: Journal entry {entryId} is unbalanced: debits {debitTotal} vs credits {creditTotal}. GAAP double-entry requires equality; correct and resubmit.

rules.name: Hold postings to closed periods
rules.description: Once a period is closed, adjustments belong in the current period (or a formally reopened one) — retroactive edits break the audit trail.
rules.reasonTemplate: Journal entry {entryId} targets a closed accounting period; post to the open period or reopen formally with approval.

rules.name: Flag material manual adjustments
rules.description: Manual (non-system) adjustments above the 100,000 materiality threshold require dual approval — the classic management-override risk.
rules.reasonTemplate: Manual journal adjustment {entryId} of {amount} exceeds the 100000 materiality threshold (prepared by {preparedBy}); dual approval required before posting.

rules.name: Approve clean journal entries
rules.description: Balanced, in-period, non-material entries post automatically with a compliance score.
rules.reasonTemplate: Journal entry {entryId} passed posting controls: balanced ({debitTotal}={creditTotal}), open period, within materiality.
